demo_name: Fred Hutch Governance Session: UC governance on OMOP

deploy_target.note: Two-step stand-up: (1) databricks bundle deploy --target client, then (2) databricks bundle run setup_clone_job --target client. The setup job DEEP CLONEs the 6 OMOP tables from source_catalog.source_schema into client_catalog.client_schema so policies are applied in ISOLATION, never on a schema another track reads. clone_omop_tables.sql does all 6.

toggle.note: No boolean toggle. The clone source is set by source_catalog/source_schema (job params to clone_omop_tables.sql). Synthetic = your own catalog + clinops_foundation (the shared foundation). Real = the FH OMOP schema, a DIFFERENT catalog (curated_omop.omop). Set BOTH source_catalog and source_schema. Table names are identical, so the same masks/filters bind either way. The notebooks apply policies to the CLONES in client_schema, so no source widget is needed in the notebooks, only the clone job reads source.

widget_config.note: The notebooks read from these dbutils.widgets, NOT from bundle vars. At setup the skill rewrites each widget's DEFAULT (2nd arg of dbutils.widgets.text) to match the bundle: catalog<-client_catalog, schema<-client_schema, warehouse_id<-warehouse_id, ocdo_group<-ocdo_group. There is NO source widget here. The notebooks operate on the clones in client_schema; the synth/real source is set only in databricks.yml and consumed by the clone job. Rewriting a widget DEFAULT is a setup action, not a rename, allowed.

real_mode.how: Set source_catalog=curated_omop and source_schema=omop in databricks.yml (targets.client.variables), then re-run setup_clone_job so the 6 tables are re-cloned from real OMOP into client_schema.
real_mode.rebuild_assets: After re-cloning from real, RE-RUN the governance notebooks (nb 01-03) so the tags, masks, and row filters are re-applied to the real clones. Policies bind to the clones in client_schema, so nothing is ever applied to the shared source, but the clones themselves must be refreshed from real and re-governed.

tables.name: person
tables.role: Cloned into the governance schema. PHI columns: person_id (identifier), year_of_birth, gender_source_value, birth_date.
tables.access: cloned into client_schema; policies applied here

tables.name: condition_occurrence
tables.role: Cloned. person_id is the row-filter key.

tables.name: measurement
tables.role: Cloned. Clinical facts (biomarkers): data_sensitivity, not phi.

tables.name: note
tables.role: Cloned. note_text is free-text PHI, masked in nb 02; person_id is an identifier.

governed_tag_policy.note: This metastore ENFORCES governed tag policies. You cannot invent tag values. Use only the allowed values from _config's PHI_COLUMNS map.

phi.description: Only accepts HIPAA Safe-Harbor identifier types.

phi_columns.note: The PHI_COLUMNS map in notebooks/_config.py is the source of truth for what gets tagged/masked. Identifiers get phi + data_sensitivity; clinical facts get data_sensitivity only (phi_type is None).

gold.note: The 6 OMOP tables are deep-cloned as-is; the team applies tags/masks/row filters on top. Governance is the common thread every section inherits. The same pattern is then applied (by Josh rotating) to the DE/ML silver/gold/models.

dependency_map.note: nb 02 column mask, gates on is_account_group_member(ocdo_group)

grain_constraints.note: Row-filter key; one row per patient

source_inputs.description: 6 OMOP tables deep-cloned into the governance schema. Synthetic default = clinops_foundation.
FH real OMOP. Point source_catalog/source_schema here to govern real data. Identical table names.

verify_queries.description: Checkpoint 1: all 6 OMOP tables present in the GOVERNANCE schema (not the source) after the clone job
verify_queries.sql: SELECT 'person' AS tbl, COUNT(*) AS n FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.person UNION ALL SELECT 'condition_occurrence', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.condition_occurrence UNION ALL SELECT 'measurement', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.measurement UNION ALL SELECT 'observation', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.observation UNION ALL SELECT 'drug_exposure', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.drug_exposure UNION ALL SELECT 'note', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.note ORDER BY tbl
verify_queries.expected: person=300, condition_occurrence=300, measurement=720, observation=720, drug_exposure=383, note=265 (in client_schema)

verify_queries.description: Checkpoint 2: every PHI column is tagged (coverage check = 0 missing)
verify_queries.sql: SELECT column_name, tag_name, tag_value FROM ${client_catalog}.information_schema.column_tags WHERE schema_name = '${client_schema}' ORDER BY table_name, column_name
verify_queries.expected: phi + data_sensitivity tags present on the PHI_COLUMNS map columns; 0 missing

verify_queries.description: Checkpoint 3: column masks bound to person.person_id, note.person_id, note.note_text
verify_queries.sql: SELECT table_name, column_name, mask_name FROM ${client_catalog}.information_schema.column_masks WHERE table_schema = '${client_schema}'

verify_queries.description: Checkpoint 4: row filter bound across the patient tables
verify_queries.sql: SELECT table_name, filter_name FROM ${client_catalog}.information_schema.row_filters WHERE table_schema = '${client_schema}'
verify_queries.expected: row filter on all 6 tables; owner sees 300 vs the consented subset an ocdo_group member sees

lock_targets.reason: OMOP CDM table names are standard and the clone + synth→real repointing depend on them.
Adaptation skill must not modify itself
CARDINAL RULE: never bind masks/row filters to the shared source schema. It changes what every other track sees. Policies bind ONLY to client_schema (the governance clone).

abac_note: Tag-based ABAC via CREATE POLICY (define once by tag, applies everywhere) is preview-gated on this metastore, kept as a STRETCH (see STRETCH.md). The GA column-mask + row-filter path already satisfies the full masking/access ask; do not block on ABAC.
checkpoints_note: Checkpoints 1-7 are documented in RUNBOOK.md; nb 05 (AI-feature governance) and nb 06 (inactive users) depend on system tables (serving usage / system.access.audit) that an account admin may need to enable.
